content.problemStatement: Write a program to input an integer N in decimal form, convert it into binary, and then print the result.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N (in decimal).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the binary representation of N without leading zeros on a separate line.

solution.approach: The binary representation of a non‑negative integer can be obtained by repeatedly dividing the number by 2 and recording the remainders. The remainders collected from last to first form the binary string. For N=0 we output "0". This process takes O(log₂N) steps per test case, which is optimal because the output itself has that length. The algorithm is straightforward, uses only integer arithmetic and a small temporary buffer, and works for all values within the constraints.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T·log N) total, where log N is the number of bits of each N
solution.spaceComplexity: O(log N) auxiliary space for the binary string of each test case

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int T; if(scanf("%d",&T)!=1) return 0; while(T--){int N; scanf("%d",&N); if(N==0){printf("0\n"); continue;} char buf[32]; int idx=0; while(N>0){buf[idx++]= (N%2)+'0'; N/=2;} for(int i=idx-1;i>=0;--i) putchar(buf[i]); putchar('\n'); } return 0;}
code.Python3:
import sys
data=sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
T=int(data[0])
out=[]
idx=1
for _ in range(T):
    N=int(data[idx]); idx+=1
    if N==0:
        out.append('0')
    else:
        bits=[]
        while N>0:
            bits.append(str(N&1))
            N>>=1
        out.append(''.join(reversed(bits)))
print('\n'.join(out))
